title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Go Semantics Case: Short Write Loop
summary: A parser assumes one Read returns a complete line, converts arbitrary bytes to text too early, loses buffered bytes, and treats a short read or write as end-of-message. Planned artifact: Add and verify the short write loop increment in fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Short Write Loop · Theory Short Write Loop
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3075 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Short Write Loop contract for the supplied data.
